Resolved coordinator topology with a concrete agent roster.

оЂЄ

agents: array of [BetaManagedAgentsAgentReference](api/beta.md) { id, type, version } о‚љ

Agents the coordinator may spawn as session threads, each resolved to a specific version.

id: stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

type: "agent"о‚љ

"agent"о‚љ

version: numberо‚љ

оЂЄ

type: "coordinator"о‚љ

"coordinator"о‚љ

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_multiagent\_coordinator\_params: object { agents, type } о‚љ

A coordinator topology: the session's primary thread orchestrates work by spawning session threads, each running an agent drawn from the `agents` roster.

оЂЄ

agents: array of [BetaManagedAgentsMultiagentRosterEntryParams](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Agents the coordinator may spawn as session threads. 1вЂ“20 entries. Each entry is an agent ID string, a versioned `{"type":"agent","id","version"}` reference, or `{"type":"self"}` to allow recursive self-invocation. Entries must reference distinct agents (after resolving `self` and string forms); at most one `self`. Referenced agents must exist, must not be archived, and must not themselves have `multiagent` set (depth limit 1).

union\_member\_0: stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_agent\_params: object { id, type, version } о‚љ

Specification for an Agent. Provide a specific `version` or use the short-form `agent="agent_id"` for the most recent version

id: stringо‚љ

The `agent` ID.

оЂЄ

type: "agent"о‚љ

"agent"о‚љ

version: optional numberо‚љ

The specific `agent` version to use. Omit to use the latest version. Must be at least 1 if specified.

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_multiagent\_self\_params: object { type } о‚љ

type: "self"о‚љ

"coordinator"о‚љ

#### BetaAgentsVersions

##### [List Agent Versions](api/beta/agents/versions/list.md)

$ ant beta:agents:versions list

GET/v1/agents/{agent\_id}/versions

#### BetaEnvironments

##### [Create Environment](api/beta/environments/create.md)

$ ant beta:environments create

POST/v1/environments

##### [List Environments](api/beta/environments/list.md)

$ ant beta:environments list

GET/v1/environments

##### [Get Environment](api/beta/environments/retrieve.md)

$ ant beta:environments retrieve

GET/v1/environments/{environment\_id}

##### [Update Environment](api/beta/environments/update.md)

$ ant beta:environments update

POST/v1/environments/{environment\_id}

##### [Delete Environment](api/beta/environments/delete.md)

$ ant beta:environments delete

DELETE/v1/environments/{environment\_id}

##### [Archive Environment](api/beta/environments/archive.md)

$ ant beta:environments archive

POST/v1/environments/{environment\_id}/archive

allow\_mcp\_servers: optional booleanо‚љ

Permits outbound access to MCP server endpoints configured on the agent, beyond those listed in the `allowed_hosts` array. Defaults to `false`.

allow\_package\_managers: optional booleanо‚љ

When versioning, use the version semantics relevant for the package manager, e.g. for `pip` use `package==1.0.0`. You are responsible for validating the package and version exist. Unversioned installs the latest.

#### BetaEnvironmentsWork

##### [Get Work Item](api/beta/environments/work/retrieve.md)

$ ant beta:environments:work retrieve

GET/v1/environments/{environment\_id}/work/{work\_id}

##### [Poll for Work](api/beta/environments/work/poll.md)

$ ant beta:environments:work poll

GET/v1/environments/{environment\_id}/work/poll

##### [Acknowledge Work](api/beta/environments/work/ack.md)

$ ant beta:environments:work ack

POST/v1/environments/{environment\_id}/work/{work\_id}/ack

##### [Record Heartbeat](api/beta/environments/work/heartbeat.md)

$ ant beta:environments:work heartbeat

POST/v1/environments/{environment\_id}/work/{work\_id}/heartbeat

##### [Stop Work](api/beta/environments/work/stop.md)

$ ant beta:environments:work stop

POST/v1/environments/{environment\_id}/work/{work\_id}/stop

##### [List Work Items](api/beta/environments/work/list.md)

$ ant beta:environments:work list

GET/v1/environments/{environment\_id}/work

##### [Update Work Item](api/beta/environments/work/update.md)

$ ant beta:environments:work update

POST/v1/environments/{environment\_id}/work/{work\_id}

##### [Get Queue Statistics](api/beta/environments/work/stats.md)

$ ant beta:environments:work stats

GET/v1/environments/{environment\_id}/work/stats

оЂЄ

beta\_self\_hosted\_work: object { id, acknowledged\_at, created\_at, 9 more } о‚љ

Work resource representing a unit of work in a self-hosted environment.

Work items are queued when sessions are created or when long-dormant sessions
receive new messages. The environment worker polls for work to execute in a
self-hosted sandbox.

id: stringо‚љ

Work identifier (e.g., 'work\_...')

acknowledged\_at: stringо‚љ

RFC 3339 timestamp when the work item was acknowledged and assigned to a self-hosted sandbox

created\_at: stringо‚љ

RFC 3339 timestamp when work was created

оЂЄ

data: object { id, type } о‚љ

The actual work to be performed

id: stringо‚љ

Session identifier (e.g., 'session\_...')

type: "session"о‚љ

Type of work data

environment\_id: stringо‚љ

Environment identifier this work belongs to (e.g., `env_...`)

latest\_heartbeat\_at: stringо‚љ

RFC 3339 timestamp of the most recent heartbeat

metadata: map[string]о‚љ

User-provided metadata key-value pairs associated with this work item

started\_at: stringо‚љ

RFC 3339 timestamp when work execution started

оЂЄ

state: "queued" or "starting" or "active" or 2 moreо‚љ

Current state of the work item

"queued"о‚љ

"starting"о‚љ

"active"о‚љ

"stopping"о‚љ

"stopped"о‚љ

stop\_requested\_at: stringо‚љ

RFC 3339 timestamp when stop was requested

stopped\_at: stringо‚љ

RFC 3339 timestamp when work execution stopped

type: "work"о‚љ

The type of object (always 'work')

оЂЄ

beta\_self\_hosted\_work\_heartbeat\_response: object { last\_heartbeat, lease\_extended, state, 2 more } о‚љ

Response after recording a heartbeat for a work item.

last\_heartbeat: stringо‚љ

RFC 3339 timestamp of the actual heartbeat from DB

lease\_extended: booleanо‚љ

Whether the heartbeat succeeded in extending the lease

оЂЄ

"starting"о‚љ

"active"о‚љ

"stopping"о‚љ

"stopped"о‚љ

beta\_self\_hosted\_work\_queue\_stats: object { depth, oldest\_queued\_at, pending, 2 more } о‚љ

Statistics about the work queue for an environment.

Uses Redis Stream consumer group metrics for O(1) queries.

depth: numberо‚љ

Number of work items waiting to be picked up (lag from consumer group)

oldest\_queued\_at: stringо‚љ

RFC 3339 timestamp of oldest item in the work stream (includes both queued and pending items), null if stream empty

pending: numberо‚љ

Number of work items being processed (polled but not acknowledged)

type: "work\_queue\_stats"о‚љ

The type of object

workers\_polling: numberо‚љ

Number of workers that have polled for work in the last 30 seconds. Requires worker\_id to be sent with poll requests.

оЂЄ

beta\_self\_hosted\_work\_stop\_request: object { force } о‚љ

Request to stop a work item.

force: optional booleanо‚љ

If true, immediately stop work without graceful shutdown

оЂЄ

beta\_self\_hosted\_work\_update\_request: object { metadata } о‚љ

Request to update work item metadata.

metadata: map[string]о‚љ

Metadata patch. Set a key to a string to upsert it, or to null to delete it. Omit the field to preserve existing metadata.

оЂЄ

beta\_session\_work\_data: object { id, type } о‚љ

Work data for session work items.

This resource type is used when work represents a session that needs to be executed
in a self-hosted environment.

id: stringо‚љ

Session identifier (e.g., 'session\_...')

type: "session"о‚љ

Type of work data

#### BetaSessions

##### [Create Session](api/beta/sessions/create.md)

$ ant beta:sessions create

POST/v1/sessions

##### [List Sessions](api/beta/sessions/list.md)

$ ant beta:sessions list

GET/v1/sessions

##### [Get Session](api/beta/sessions/retrieve.md)

$ ant beta:sessions retrieve

GET/v1/sessions/{session\_id}

##### [Update Session](api/beta/sessions/update.md)

$ ant beta:sessions update

POST/v1/sessions/{session\_id}

##### [Delete Session](api/beta/sessions/delete.md)

$ ant beta:sessions delete

DELETE/v1/sessions/{session\_id}

##### [Archive Session](api/beta/sessions/archive.md)

$ ant beta:sessions archive

POST/v1/sessions/{session\_id}/archive

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_multiagent\_params: object { agents, type } о‚љ

A coordinator topology: the session's primary thread orchestrates work by spawning session threads, each running an agent drawn from the `agents` roster.

оЂЄ

agents: array of [BetaManagedAgentsMultiagentRosterEntryParams](api/beta.md)о‚љ

Agents the coordinator may spawn as session threads. 1вЂ“20 entries. Each entry is an agent ID string, a versioned `{"type":"agent","id","version"}` reference, or `{"type":"self"}` to allow recursive self-invocation. Entries must reference distinct agents (after resolving `self` and string forms); at most one `self`. Referenced agents must exist, must not be archived, and must not themselves have `multiagent` set (depth limit 1).

union\_member\_0: stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_outcome\_evaluation\_resource: object { completed\_at, description, explanation, 4 more } о‚љ

Evaluation state for a single outcome defined via a define\_outcome event.

completed\_at: stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

description: stringо‚љ

What the agent should produce.

explanation: stringо‚љ

Grader's verdict text from the most recent evaluation. For satisfied, explains why criteria are met; for needs\_revision (intermediate), what's missing; for failed, why unrecoverable.

iteration: numberо‚љ

0-indexed revision cycle the outcome is currently on.

outcome\_id: stringо‚љ

Server-generated outc\_ ID for this outcome.

result: stringо‚љ

Current evaluation state. `pending` before the agent begins work; `running` while producing or revising; `evaluating` while the grader scores; `satisfied`/`max_iterations_reached`/`failed`/`interrupted` are terminal.

оЂЄ

type: "outcome\_evaluation"о‚љ

"outcome\_evaluation"о‚љ

outcome\_evaluations: array of [BetaManagedAgentsOutcomeEvaluationResource](api/beta.md) { completed\_at, description, explanation, 4 more } о‚љ

Per-outcome evaluation state. One entry per define\_outcome event sent to the session.

completed\_at: stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

description: stringо‚љ

What the agent should produce.

explanation: stringо‚љ

Grader's verdict text from the most recent evaluation. For satisfied, explains why criteria are met; for needs\_revision (intermediate), what's missing; for failed, why unrecoverable.

iteration: numberо‚љ

0-indexed revision cycle the outcome is currently on.

outcome\_id: stringо‚љ

Server-generated outc\_ ID for this outcome.

result: stringо‚љ

Current evaluation state. `pending` before the agent begins work; `running` while producing or revising; `evaluating` while the grader scores; `satisfied`/`max_iterations_reached`/`failed`/`interrupted` are terminal.

оЂЄ

type: "outcome\_evaluation"о‚љ

"outcome\_evaluation"о‚љ

beta\_managed\_agents\_memory\_store\_resource: object { memory\_store\_id, type, access, 4 more } о‚љ

A memory store attached to an agent session.

memory\_store\_id: stringо‚љ

The memory store ID (memstore\_...). Must belong to the caller's organization and workspace.

оЂЄ

type: "memory\_store"о‚љ

"memory\_store"о‚љ

оЂЄ

access: optional "read\_write" or "read\_only"о‚љ

Access mode for an attached memory store.

"read\_write"о‚љ

"read\_only"о‚љ

description: optional stringо‚љ

Description of the memory store, snapshotted at attach time. Rendered into the agent's system prompt. Empty string when the store has no description.

instructions: optional stringо‚љ

Per-attachment guidance for the agent on how to use this store. Rendered into the memory section of the system prompt. Max 4096 chars.

mount\_path: optional stringо‚љ

Filesystem path where the store is mounted in the session container, e.g. /mnt/memory/user-preferences. Derived from the store's name. Output-only.

name: optional stringо‚љ

Display name of the memory store, snapshotted at attach time. Later edits to the store's name do not propagate to this resource.

оЂЄ

stats: object { active\_seconds, duration\_seconds } о‚љ

Timing statistics for a session.

active\_seconds: optional numberо‚љ

Cumulative time in seconds the session spent in running status. Excludes idle time.

duration\_seconds: optional numberо‚љ

Elapsed time since session creation in seconds. For terminated sessions, frozen at the final update.

оЂЄ

status: "rescheduling" or "running" or "idle" or "terminated"о‚љ

SessionStatus enum

"rescheduling"о‚љ

"running"о‚љ

"terminated"о‚љ

usage: object { cache\_creation, cache\_read\_input\_tokens, input\_tokens, output\_tokens } о‚љ

Cumulative token usage for a session across all turns.

оЂЄ

cache\_creation: optional object { ephemeral\_1h\_input\_tokens, ephemeral\_5m\_input\_tokens } о‚љ

Prompt-cache creation token usage broken down by cache lifetime.

ephemeral\_1h\_input\_tokens: optional numberо‚љ

Tokens used to create 1-hour ephemeral cache entries.

ephemeral\_5m\_input\_tokens: optional numberо‚љ

Tokens used to create 5-minute ephemeral cache entries.

cache\_read\_input\_tokens: optional numberо‚љ

Total tokens read from prompt cache.

input\_tokens: optional numberо‚љ

Total input tokens consumed across all turns.

output\_tokens: optional numberо‚љ

Total output tokens generated across all turns.

vault\_ids: array of stringо‚љ

Vault IDs attached to the session at creation. Empty when no vaults were supplied.

deployment\_id: optional stringо‚љ

Deployment ID when the session was created from a deployment reference. Null otherwise.

beta\_managed\_agents\_session\_agent\_update: object { mcp\_servers, tools } о‚љ

Mid-session agent configuration update. Only `tools` and `mcp_servers` are updatable. Full replacement: the provided array becomes the new value. To preserve existing entries, GET the session, modify the array, and POST it back.

оЂЄ

mcp\_servers: optional array of [BetaManagedAgentsURLMCPServerParams](api/beta.md) { name, type, url } о‚љ

Replacement MCP server list. Full replacement: the provided array becomes the new value. Send an empty array to clear; omit to preserve.

name: stringо‚љ

Unique name for this server, referenced by mcp\_toolset configurations. 1-255 characters.

оЂЄ

type: "url"о‚љ

"url"о‚љ

url: stringо‚љ

Endpoint URL for the MCP server.

оЂЄ

tools: optional array of [BetaManagedAgentsAgentToolset20260401Params](api/beta.md) { type, configs, default\_config }  or [BetaManagedAgentsMCPToolsetParams](api/beta.md) { mcp\_server\_name, type, configs, default\_config }  or [BetaManagedAgentsCustomToolParams](api/beta.md) { description, input\_schema, name, type } о‚љ

Replacement tool list. Full replacement: the provided array becomes the new value. Send an empty array to clear; omit to preserve.

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_agent\_toolset20260401\_params: object { type, configs, default\_config } о‚љ

Configuration for built-in agent tools. Use this to enable or disable groups of tools available to the agent.

оЂЄ

type: "agent\_toolset\_20260401"о‚љ

"agent\_toolset\_20260401"о‚љ

оЂЄ

configs: optional array of [BetaManagedAgentsAgentToolConfigParams](api/beta.md) { name, enabled, permission\_policy } о‚љ

beta\_managed\_agents\_custom\_tool\_params: object { description, input\_schema, name, type } о‚љ

A custom tool that is executed by the API client rather than the agent. When the agent calls this tool, an `agent.custom_tool_use` event is emitted and the session goes idle, waiting for the client to provide the result via a `user.custom_tool_result` event.

description: stringо‚љ

Description of what the tool does, shown to the agent to help it decide when to use the tool. 1-1024 characters.

оЂЄ

input\_schema: object { type, properties, required } о‚љ

JSON Schema for custom tool input parameters.

type: "object"о‚љ

properties: optional map[unknown]о‚љ

required: optional array of stringо‚љ

name: stringо‚љ

Unique name for the tool. 1-128 characters; letters, digits, underscores, and hyphens.

оЂЄ

type: "custom"о‚љ

"custom"о‚љ

Timing statistics for a session.

active\_seconds: optional numberо‚љ

Cumulative time in seconds the session spent in running status. Excludes idle time.

duration\_seconds: optional numberо‚љ

Elapsed time since session creation in seconds. For terminated sessions, frozen at the final update.

Emitted when an UpdateSession request changed at least one field. Carries only the fields that changed; absent fields were not part of the update. The new configuration applies from the next turn.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

processed\_at: stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

оЂЄ

Prompt-cache creation token usage broken down by cache lifetime.

ephemeral\_5m\_input\_tokens: optional numberо‚љ

#### BetaSessionsEvents

##### [List Events](api/beta/sessions/events/list.md)

$ ant beta:sessions:events list

GET/v1/sessions/{session\_id}/events

##### [Send Events](api/beta/sessions/events/send.md)

$ ant beta:sessions:events send

POST/v1/sessions/{session\_id}/events

##### [Stream Events](api/beta/sessions/events/stream.md)

$ ant beta:sessions:events stream

GET/v1/sessions/{session\_id}/events/stream

##### ModelsExpand Collapse

beta\_managed\_agents\_agent\_custom\_tool\_use\_event: object { id, input, name, 3 more } о‚љ

Event emitted when the agent calls a custom tool. The session goes idle until the client sends a `user.custom_tool_result` event with the result.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

input: map[unknown]о‚љ

Input parameters for the tool call.

name: stringо‚љ

Name of the custom tool being called.

processed\_at: stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

оЂЄ

type: "agent.custom\_tool\_use"о‚љ

"agent.custom\_tool\_use"о‚љ

session\_thread\_id: optional stringо‚љ

When set, this event was cross-posted from a subagent's thread to surface its custom tool use on the primary thread's stream. Empty on the thread's own events. Echo this on a `user.custom_tool_result` event to route the result back.

beta\_managed\_agents\_billing\_error: object { message, retry\_status, type } о‚љ

The caller's organization or workspace cannot make model requests вЂ” out of credits or spend limit reached. Retrying with the same credentials will not succeed; the caller must resolve the billing state.

message: stringо‚љ

Human-readable error description.

оЂЄ

retry\_status: [BetaManagedAgentsRetryStatusRetrying](api/beta.md) { type }  or [BetaManagedAgentsRetryStatusExhausted](api/beta.md) { type }  or [BetaManagedAgentsRetryStatusTerminal](api/beta.md) { type } о‚љ

What the client should do next in response to this error.

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_retry\_status\_retrying: object { type } о‚љ

The server is retrying automatically. Client should wait; the same error type may fire again as retrying, then once as exhausted when the retry budget runs out.

оЂЄ

type: "retrying"о‚љ

"retrying"о‚љ

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_retry\_status\_exhausted: object { type } о‚љ

This turn is dead; queued inputs are flushed and the session returns to idle. Client may send a new prompt.

оЂЄ

type: "exhausted"о‚љ

"exhausted"о‚љ

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_retry\_status\_terminal: object { type } о‚љ

The session encountered a terminal error and will transition to `terminated` state.

оЂЄ

type: "terminal"о‚љ

"terminal"о‚љ

оЂЄ

type: "billing\_error"о‚љ

"billing\_error"о‚љ

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_system\_message\_event\_params: object { content, type } о‚љ

Privileged context for the accompanying turn and all subsequent turns, appended to the session's system context as a `role: "system"` turn rather than replacing the top-level system prompt. At most one per request: it must be the final event and immediately follow the `user.message`, `user.tool_result`, or `user.custom_tool_result` it accompanies. Only supported on models that accept mid-conversation system messages.

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_user\_interrupt\_event: object { id, type, processed\_at, session\_thread\_id } о‚љ

An interrupt event that pauses agent execution and returns control to the user.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

оЂЄ

type: "user.interrupt"о‚љ

"user.interrupt"о‚љ

processed\_at: optional stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

session\_thread\_id: optional stringо‚љ

If absent, interrupts every non-archived thread in a multiagent session (or the primary alone in a single-agent session). If present, interrupts only the named thread.

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_user\_tool\_confirmation\_event: object { id, result, tool\_use\_id, 4 more } о‚љ

A tool confirmation event that approves or denies a pending tool execution.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

оЂЄ

result: "allow" or "deny"о‚љ

UserToolConfirmationResult enum

"allow"о‚љ

"deny"о‚љ

tool\_use\_id: stringо‚љ

The id of the `agent.tool_use` or `agent.mcp_tool_use` event this result corresponds to, which can be found in the last `session.status_idle` [event's](api/beta/sessions/events/list.md) `stop_reason.event_ids` field.

оЂЄ

type: "user.tool\_confirmation"о‚љ

"user.tool\_confirmation"о‚љ

deny\_message: optional stringо‚љ

Optional message providing context for a 'deny' decision. Only allowed when result is 'deny'.

processed\_at: optional stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

session\_thread\_id: optional stringо‚љ

When set, the confirmation routes to this subagent's thread rather than the primary. Echo this from the `session_thread_id` on the `agent.tool_use` or `agent.mcp_tool_use` event that prompted the approval.

оЂЄ

Echo of a `user.define_outcome` input event. Carries the server-generated `outcome_id` that subsequent `span.outcome_evaluation_*` events reference.

Unique identifier for this event.

description: stringо‚љ

What the agent should produce. Copied from the input event.

Evaluate-then-revise cycles before giving up. Default 3, max 20.

Server-generated `outc_` ID for this outcome. Referenced by `span.outcome_evaluation_*` events and the session's `outcome_evaluations` list.

beta\_managed\_agents\_user\_interrupt\_event: object { id, type, processed\_at, session\_thread\_id } о‚љ

An interrupt event that pauses agent execution and returns control to the user.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

оЂЄ

type: "user.interrupt"о‚љ

"user.interrupt"о‚љ

processed\_at: optional stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

session\_thread\_id: optional stringо‚љ

If absent, interrupts every non-archived thread in a multiagent session (or the primary alone in a single-agent session). If present, interrupts only the named thread.

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_user\_tool\_confirmation\_event: object { id, result, tool\_use\_id, 4 more } о‚љ

A tool confirmation event that approves or denies a pending tool execution.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

оЂЄ

result: "allow" or "deny"о‚љ

UserToolConfirmationResult enum

"allow"о‚љ

"deny"о‚љ

tool\_use\_id: stringо‚љ

The id of the `agent.tool_use` or `agent.mcp_tool_use` event this result corresponds to, which can be found in the last `session.status_idle` [event's](api/beta/sessions/events/list.md) `stop_reason.event_ids` field.

оЂЄ

type: "user.tool\_confirmation"о‚љ

"user.tool\_confirmation"о‚љ

deny\_message: optional stringо‚љ

Optional message providing context for a 'deny' decision. Only allowed when result is 'deny'.

processed\_at: optional stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

session\_thread\_id: optional stringо‚љ

When set, the confirmation routes to this subagent's thread rather than the primary. Echo this from the `session_thread_id` on the `agent.tool_use` or `agent.mcp_tool_use` event that prompted the approval.

оЂЄ

Whether the tool execution resulted in an error.

processed\_at: optional stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

session\_thread\_id: optional stringо‚љ

Routes this result to a subagent thread. Copy from the `agent.custom_tool_use` event's `session_thread_id`.

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_agent\_custom\_tool\_use\_event: object { id, input, name, 3 more } о‚љ

Event emitted when the agent calls a custom tool. The session goes idle until the client sends a `user.custom_tool_result` event with the result.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

input: map[unknown]о‚љ

Input parameters for the tool call.

name: stringо‚љ

Name of the custom tool being called.

processed\_at: stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

оЂЄ

type: "agent.custom\_tool\_use"о‚љ

"agent.custom\_tool\_use"о‚љ

session\_thread\_id: optional stringо‚љ

When set, this event was cross-posted from a subagent's thread to surface its custom tool use on the primary thread's stream. Empty on the thread's own events. Echo this on a `user.custom_tool_result` event to route the result back.

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_span\_outcome\_evaluation\_start\_event: object { id, iteration, outcome\_id, 2 more } о‚љ

Emitted when an outcome evaluation cycle begins.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

iteration: numberо‚љ

0-indexed revision cycle. 0 is the first evaluation; 1 is the re-evaluation after the first revision; etc.

outcome\_id: stringо‚љ

The `outc_` ID of the outcome being evaluated.

processed\_at: stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

оЂЄ

type: "span.outcome\_evaluation\_start"о‚љ

"span.outcome\_evaluation\_start"о‚љ

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_span\_outcome\_evaluation\_end\_event: object { id, explanation, iteration, 6 more } о‚љ

Emitted when an outcome evaluation cycle completes. Carries the verdict and aggregate token usage. A verdict of `needs_revision` means another evaluation cycle follows; `satisfied`, `max_iterations_reached`, `failed`, or `interrupted` are terminal вЂ” no further evaluation cycles follow.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

explanation: stringо‚љ

Human-readable explanation of the verdict. For `needs_revision`, describes which criteria failed and why.

iteration: numberо‚љ

0-indexed revision cycle, matching the corresponding `span.outcome_evaluation_start`.

outcome\_evaluation\_start\_id: stringо‚љ

The id of the corresponding `span.outcome_evaluation_start` event.

outcome\_id: stringо‚љ

The `outc_` ID of the outcome being evaluated.

processed\_at: stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

result: stringо‚љ

Evaluation verdict. 'satisfied': criteria met, session goes idle. 'needs\_revision': criteria not met, another revision cycle follows. 'max\_iterations\_reached': evaluation budget exhausted with criteria still unmet вЂ” one final acknowledgment turn follows before the session goes idle, but no further evaluation runs. 'failed': grader determined the rubric does not apply to the deliverables. 'interrupted': user sent an interrupt while evaluation was in progress.

оЂЄ

type: "span.outcome\_evaluation\_end"о‚љ

"span.outcome\_evaluation\_end"о‚љ

оЂЄ

usage: object { cache\_creation\_input\_tokens, cache\_read\_input\_tokens, input\_tokens, 2 more } о‚љ

Token usage for a single model request.

cache\_creation\_input\_tokens: numberо‚љ

Tokens used to create prompt cache in this request.

cache\_read\_input\_tokens: numberо‚љ

Tokens read from prompt cache in this request.

input\_tokens: numberо‚љ

Input tokens consumed by this request.

output\_tokens: numberо‚љ

Output tokens generated by this request.

оЂЄ

speed: optional "standard" or "fast"о‚љ

Inference speed mode. `fast` provides significantly faster output token generation at premium pricing. Not all models support `fast`; invalid combinations are rejected at create time.

"standard"о‚љ

"fast"о‚љ

оЂЄ

iteration: numberо‚љ

beta\_managed\_agents\_user\_define\_outcome\_event: object { id, description, max\_iterations, 4 more } о‚љ

Echo of a `user.define_outcome` input event. Carries the server-generated `outcome_id` that subsequent `span.outcome_evaluation_*` events reference.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

description: stringо‚љ

What the agent should produce. Copied from the input event.

max\_iterations: numberо‚љ

Evaluate-then-revise cycles before giving up. Default 3, max 20.

outcome\_id: stringо‚љ

Server-generated `outc_` ID for this outcome. Referenced by `span.outcome_evaluation_*` events and the session's `outcome_evaluations` list.

processed\_at: stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

beta\_managed\_agents\_session\_thread\_status\_idle\_event: object { id, agent\_name, processed\_at, 3 more } о‚љ

A session thread has yielded and is awaiting input. Emitted on the thread's own stream and cross-posted to the primary stream for child threads.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

agent\_name: stringо‚љ

Name of the agent the thread runs.

processed\_at: stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

session\_thread\_id: stringо‚љ

Public sthr\_ ID of the thread that went idle.

оЂЄ

stop\_reason: [BetaManagedAgentsSessionEndTurn](api/beta.md) { type }  or [BetaManagedAgentsSessionRequiresAction](api/beta.md) { event\_ids, type }  or [BetaManagedAgentsSessionRetriesExhausted](api/beta.md) { type } о‚љ

The agent completed its turn naturally and is ready for the next user message.

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_session\_end\_turn: object { type } о‚љ

The agent completed its turn naturally and is ready for the next user message.

оЂЄ

type: "end\_turn"о‚љ

"end\_turn"о‚љ

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_session\_requires\_action: object { event\_ids, type } о‚љ

The agent is idle waiting on one or more blocking user-input events (tool confirmation, custom tool result, etc.). Resolving all of them transitions the session back to running.

event\_ids: array of stringо‚љ

The ids of events the agent is blocked on. Resolving fewer than all re-emits `session.status_idle` with the remainder.

оЂЄ

type: "requires\_action"о‚љ

"requires\_action"о‚љ

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_session\_retries\_exhausted: object { type } о‚љ

The turn ended because the retry budget was exhausted (`max_iterations` hit or an error escalated to `retry_status: 'exhausted'`).

оЂЄ

type: "retries\_exhausted"о‚љ

"retries\_exhausted"о‚љ

оЂЄ

type: "session.thread\_status\_idle"о‚љ

"session.thread\_status\_idle"о‚љ

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_session\_thread\_status\_terminated\_event: object { id, agent\_name, processed\_at, 2 more } о‚љ

A session thread has terminated and will accept no further input. Emitted on the thread's own stream and cross-posted to the primary stream for child threads.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

agent\_name: stringо‚љ

Name of the agent the thread runs.

processed\_at: stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

session\_thread\_id: stringо‚љ

Public sthr\_ ID of the thread that terminated.

оЂЄ

type: "session.thread\_status\_terminated"о‚љ

"session.thread\_status\_terminated"о‚љ

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_user\_tool\_result\_event: object { id, tool\_use\_id, type, 4 more } о‚љ

Event sent by the client providing the result of an agent-toolset tool execution. Only valid on `self_hosted` environments, where sandbox-routed tools are executed by the client rather than the server.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

tool\_use\_id: stringо‚љ

The id of the `agent.tool_use` event this result corresponds to, which can be found in the last `session.status_idle` [event's](api/beta/sessions/events/list.md) `stop_reason.event_ids` field.

оЂЄ

type: "user.tool\_result"о‚љ

"user.tool\_result"о‚љ

"claude-opus-4-8"о‚љ

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_span\_outcome\_evaluation\_end\_event: object { id, explanation, iteration, 6 more } о‚љ

Emitted when an outcome evaluation cycle completes. Carries the verdict and aggregate token usage. A verdict of `needs_revision` means another evaluation cycle follows; `satisfied`, `max_iterations_reached`, `failed`, or `interrupted` are terminal вЂ” no further evaluation cycles follow.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

explanation: stringо‚љ

Human-readable explanation of the verdict. For `needs_revision`, describes which criteria failed and why.

iteration: numberо‚љ

0-indexed revision cycle, matching the corresponding `span.outcome_evaluation_start`.

outcome\_evaluation\_start\_id: stringо‚љ

The id of the corresponding `span.outcome_evaluation_start` event.

outcome\_id: stringо‚љ

The `outc_` ID of the outcome being evaluated.

processed\_at: stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

result: stringо‚љ

Evaluation verdict. 'satisfied': criteria met, session goes idle. 'needs\_revision': criteria not met, another revision cycle follows. 'max\_iterations\_reached': evaluation budget exhausted with criteria still unmet вЂ” one final acknowledgment turn follows before the session goes idle, but no further evaluation runs. 'failed': grader determined the rubric does not apply to the deliverables. 'interrupted': user sent an interrupt while evaluation was in progress.

оЂЄ

type: "span.outcome\_evaluation\_end"о‚љ

"span.outcome\_evaluation\_end"о‚љ

оЂЄ

usage: object { cache\_creation\_input\_tokens, cache\_read\_input\_tokens, input\_tokens, 2 more } о‚љ

Token usage for a single model request.

cache\_creation\_input\_tokens: numberо‚љ

Tokens used to create prompt cache in this request.

cache\_read\_input\_tokens: numberо‚љ

Tokens read from prompt cache in this request.

input\_tokens: numberо‚љ

Input tokens consumed by this request.

output\_tokens: numberо‚љ

Output tokens generated by this request.

оЂЄ

speed: optional "standard" or "fast"о‚љ

Inference speed mode. `fast` provides significantly faster output token generation at premium pricing. Not all models support `fast`; invalid combinations are rejected at create time.

"standard"о‚љ

"fast"о‚љ

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_span\_outcome\_evaluation\_ongoing\_event: object { id, iteration, outcome\_id, 2 more } о‚љ

Periodic heartbeat emitted while an outcome evaluation cycle is in progress. Distinguishes 'evaluation is actively running' from 'evaluation is stuck' between the corresponding `span.outcome_evaluation_start` and `span.outcome_evaluation_end` events.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

iteration: numberо‚љ

0-indexed revision cycle, matching the corresponding `span.outcome_evaluation_start`.

outcome\_id: stringо‚љ

The `outc_` ID of the outcome being evaluated.

processed\_at: stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

оЂЄ

type: "span.outcome\_evaluation\_ongoing"о‚љ

"span.outcome\_evaluation\_ongoing"о‚љ

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_span\_outcome\_evaluation\_start\_event: object { id, iteration, outcome\_id, 2 more } о‚љ

Emitted when an outcome evaluation cycle begins.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

iteration: numberо‚љ

0-indexed revision cycle. 0 is the first evaluation; 1 is the re-evaluation after the first revision; etc.

outcome\_id: stringо‚љ

The `outc_` ID of the outcome being evaluated.

processed\_at: stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

оЂЄ

type: "span.outcome\_evaluation\_start"о‚љ

"span.outcome\_evaluation\_start"о‚љ

beta\_managed\_agents\_user\_interrupt\_event: object { id, type, processed\_at, session\_thread\_id } о‚љ

An interrupt event that pauses agent execution and returns control to the user.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

оЂЄ

type: "user.interrupt"о‚љ

"user.interrupt"о‚љ

processed\_at: optional stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

session\_thread\_id: optional stringо‚љ

If absent, interrupts every non-archived thread in a multiagent session (or the primary alone in a single-agent session). If present, interrupts only the named thread.

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_user\_tool\_confirmation\_event: object { id, result, tool\_use\_id, 4 more } о‚љ

A tool confirmation event that approves or denies a pending tool execution.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

оЂЄ

result: "allow" or "deny"о‚љ

UserToolConfirmationResult enum

"allow"о‚љ

"deny"о‚љ

tool\_use\_id: stringо‚љ

The id of the `agent.tool_use` or `agent.mcp_tool_use` event this result corresponds to, which can be found in the last `session.status_idle` [event's](api/beta/sessions/events/list.md) `stop_reason.event_ids` field.

оЂЄ

type: "user.tool\_confirmation"о‚љ

"user.tool\_confirmation"о‚љ

deny\_message: optional stringо‚љ

Optional message providing context for a 'deny' decision. Only allowed when result is 'deny'.

processed\_at: optional stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

session\_thread\_id: optional stringо‚љ

When set, the confirmation routes to this subagent's thread rather than the primary. Echo this from the `session_thread_id` on the `agent.tool_use` or `agent.mcp_tool_use` event that prompted the approval.

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_agent\_custom\_tool\_use\_event: object { id, input, name, 3 more } о‚љ

Event emitted when the agent calls a custom tool. The session goes idle until the client sends a `user.custom_tool_result` event with the result.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

input: map[unknown]о‚љ

Input parameters for the tool call.

name: stringо‚љ

Name of the custom tool being called.

processed\_at: stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

оЂЄ

type: "agent.custom\_tool\_use"о‚љ

"agent.custom\_tool\_use"о‚љ

session\_thread\_id: optional stringо‚љ

When set, this event was cross-posted from a subagent's thread to surface its custom tool use on the primary thread's stream. Empty on the thread's own events. Echo this on a `user.custom_tool_result` event to route the result back.

оЂЄ

id: stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

beta\_managed\_agents\_span\_outcome\_evaluation\_start\_event: object { id, iteration, outcome\_id, 2 more } о‚љ

Emitted when an outcome evaluation cycle begins.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

iteration: numberо‚љ

0-indexed revision cycle. 0 is the first evaluation; 1 is the re-evaluation after the first revision; etc.

outcome\_id: stringо‚љ

The `outc_` ID of the outcome being evaluated.

processed\_at: stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

оЂЄ

type: "span.outcome\_evaluation\_start"о‚љ

"span.outcome\_evaluation\_start"о‚љ

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_span\_outcome\_evaluation\_end\_event: object { id, explanation, iteration, 6 more } о‚љ

Emitted when an outcome evaluation cycle completes. Carries the verdict and aggregate token usage. A verdict of `needs_revision` means another evaluation cycle follows; `satisfied`, `max_iterations_reached`, `failed`, or `interrupted` are terminal вЂ” no further evaluation cycles follow.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

explanation: stringо‚љ

Human-readable explanation of the verdict. For `needs_revision`, describes which criteria failed and why.

iteration: numberо‚љ

0-indexed revision cycle, matching the corresponding `span.outcome_evaluation_start`.

outcome\_evaluation\_start\_id: stringо‚љ

The id of the corresponding `span.outcome_evaluation_start` event.

outcome\_id: stringо‚љ

The `outc_` ID of the outcome being evaluated.

processed\_at: stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

result: stringо‚љ

Evaluation verdict. 'satisfied': criteria met, session goes idle. 'needs\_revision': criteria not met, another revision cycle follows. 'max\_iterations\_reached': evaluation budget exhausted with criteria still unmet вЂ” one final acknowledgment turn follows before the session goes idle, but no further evaluation runs. 'failed': grader determined the rubric does not apply to the deliverables. 'interrupted': user sent an interrupt while evaluation was in progress.

оЂЄ

type: "span.outcome\_evaluation\_end"о‚љ

"span.outcome\_evaluation\_end"о‚љ

оЂЄ

usage: object { cache\_creation\_input\_tokens, cache\_read\_input\_tokens, input\_tokens, 2 more } о‚љ

Token usage for a single model request.

cache\_creation\_input\_tokens: numberо‚љ

Tokens used to create prompt cache in this request.

cache\_read\_input\_tokens: numberо‚љ

Tokens read from prompt cache in this request.

input\_tokens: numberо‚љ

Input tokens consumed by this request.

output\_tokens: numberо‚љ

Output tokens generated by this request.

оЂЄ

speed: optional "standard" or "fast"о‚љ

Inference speed mode. `fast` provides significantly faster output token generation at premium pricing. Not all models support `fast`; invalid combinations are rejected at create time.

"standard"о‚љ

"fast"о‚љ

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_span\_outcome\_evaluation\_ongoing\_event: object { id, iteration, outcome\_id, 2 more } о‚љ

Periodic heartbeat emitted while an outcome evaluation cycle is in progress. Distinguishes 'evaluation is actively running' from 'evaluation is stuck' between the corresponding `span.outcome_evaluation_start` and `span.outcome_evaluation_end` events.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

iteration: numberо‚љ

0-indexed revision cycle, matching the corresponding `span.outcome_evaluation_start`.

outcome\_id: stringо‚љ

The `outc_` ID of the outcome being evaluated.

processed\_at: stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

оЂЄ

type: "span.outcome\_evaluation\_ongoing"о‚љ

"span.outcome\_evaluation\_ongoing"о‚љ

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_user\_define\_outcome\_event: object { id, description, max\_iterations, 4 more } о‚љ

Echo of a `user.define_outcome` input event. Carries the server-generated `outcome_id` that subsequent `span.outcome_evaluation_*` events reference.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

description: stringо‚љ

What the agent should produce. Copied from the input event.

max\_iterations: numberо‚љ

Evaluate-then-revise cycles before giving up. Default 3, max 20.

outcome\_id: stringо‚љ

Server-generated `outc_` ID for this outcome. Referenced by `span.outcome_evaluation_*` events and the session's `outcome_evaluations` list.

processed\_at: stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

оЂЄ

"file"о‚љ

Rubric content provided inline as text.

Unique identifier for this event.

Name of the agent the thread runs.

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

"session.thread\_status\_running"о‚љ

beta\_managed\_agents\_session\_thread\_status\_idle\_event: object { id, agent\_name, processed\_at, 3 more } о‚љ

A session thread has yielded and is awaiting input. Emitted on the thread's own stream and cross-posted to the primary stream for child threads.

Name of the agent the thread runs.

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

beta\_managed\_agents\_session\_requires\_action: object { event\_ids, type } о‚љ

The agent is idle waiting on one or more blocking user-input events (tool confirmation, custom tool result, etc.). Resolving all of them transitions the session back to running.

The ids of events the agent is blocked on. Resolving fewer than all re-emits `session.status_idle` with the remainder.

"requires\_action"о‚љ

beta\_managed\_agents\_system\_message\_event\_params: object { content, type } о‚љ

Privileged context for the accompanying turn and all subsequent turns, appended to the session's system context as a `role: "system"` turn rather than replacing the top-level system prompt. At most one per request: it must be the final event and immediately follow the `user.message`, `user.tool_result`, or `user.custom_tool_result` it accompanies. Only supported on models that accept mid-conversation system messages.

оЂЄ

content: array of [BetaManagedAgentsSystemContentBlock](api/beta.md) { text, type } о‚љ

System content blocks to append. Text-only.

text: stringо‚љ

The text content.

beta\_managed\_agents\_unknown\_error: object { message, retry\_status, type } о‚љ

An unknown or unexpected error occurred during session execution. A fallback variant; clients that don't recognize a new error code can match on `retry_status` and `message` alone.

message: stringо‚љ

Human-readable error description.

оЂЄ

retry\_status: [BetaManagedAgentsRetryStatusRetrying](api/beta.md) { type }  or [BetaManagedAgentsRetryStatusExhausted](api/beta.md) { type }  or [BetaManagedAgentsRetryStatusTerminal](api/beta.md) { type } о‚љ

What the client should do next in response to this error.

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_retry\_status\_retrying: object { type } о‚љ

The server is retrying automatically. Client should wait; the same error type may fire again as retrying, then once as exhausted when the retry budget runs out.

оЂЄ

type: "retrying"о‚љ

"retrying"о‚љ

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_retry\_status\_exhausted: object { type } о‚љ

This turn is dead; queued inputs are flushed and the session returns to idle. Client may send a new prompt.

type: "exhausted"о‚љ

"exhausted"о‚љ

"terminal"о‚љ

"unknown\_error"о‚љ

description: stringо‚љ

Evaluate-then-revise cycles before giving up. Default 3, max 20.

beta\_managed\_agents\_user\_interrupt\_event: object { id, type, processed\_at, session\_thread\_id } о‚љ

An interrupt event that pauses agent execution and returns control to the user.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

оЂЄ

type: "user.interrupt"о‚љ

"user.interrupt"о‚љ

processed\_at: optional stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

session\_thread\_id: optional stringо‚љ

If absent, interrupts every non-archived thread in a multiagent session (or the primary alone in a single-agent session). If present, interrupts only the named thread.

beta\_managed\_agents\_user\_interrupt\_event\_params: object { type, session\_thread\_id } о‚љ

"user.interrupt"о‚љ

beta\_managed\_agents\_user\_tool\_confirmation\_event: object { id, result, tool\_use\_id, 4 more } о‚љ

A tool confirmation event that approves or denies a pending tool execution.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

оЂЄ

result: "allow" or "deny"о‚љ

UserToolConfirmationResult enum

"allow"о‚љ

"deny"о‚љ

tool\_use\_id: stringо‚љ

The id of the `agent.tool_use` or `agent.mcp_tool_use` event this result corresponds to, which can be found in the last `session.status_idle` [event's](api/beta/sessions/events/list.md) `stop_reason.event_ids` field.

оЂЄ

type: "user.tool\_confirmation"о‚љ

"user.tool\_confirmation"о‚љ

deny\_message: optional stringо‚љ

Optional message providing context for a 'deny' decision. Only allowed when result is 'deny'.

processed\_at: optional stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

session\_thread\_id: optional stringо‚љ

When set, the confirmation routes to this subagent's thread rather than the primary. Echo this from the `session_thread_id` on the `agent.tool_use` or `agent.mcp_tool_use` event that prompted the approval.

оЂЄ

"deny"о‚љ

#### BetaSessionsResources

##### [Add Session Resource](api/beta/sessions/resources/add.md)

$ ant beta:sessions:resources add

POST/v1/sessions/{session\_id}/resources

##### [List Session Resources](api/beta/sessions/resources/list.md)

$ ant beta:sessions:resources list

GET/v1/sessions/{session\_id}/resources

##### [Get Session Resource](api/beta/sessions/resources/retrieve.md)

$ ant beta:sessions:resources retrieve

GET/v1/sessions/{session\_id}/resources/{resource\_id}

##### [Update Session Resource](api/beta/sessions/resources/update.md)

$ ant beta:sessions:resources update

POST/v1/sessions/{session\_id}/resources/{resource\_id}

##### [Delete Session Resource](api/beta/sessions/resources/delete.md)

$ ant beta:sessions:resources delete

DELETE/v1/sessions/{session\_id}/resources/{resource\_id}

beta\_managed\_agents\_memory\_store\_resource: object { memory\_store\_id, type, access, 4 more } о‚љ

A memory store attached to an agent session.

memory\_store\_id: stringо‚љ

The memory store ID (memstore\_...). Must belong to the caller's organization and workspace.

оЂЄ

type: "memory\_store"о‚љ

"memory\_store"о‚љ

оЂЄ

access: optional "read\_write" or "read\_only"о‚љ

Access mode for an attached memory store.

"read\_write"о‚љ

"read\_only"о‚љ

description: optional stringо‚љ

Description of the memory store, snapshotted at attach time. Rendered into the agent's system prompt. Empty string when the store has no description.

instructions: optional stringо‚љ

Per-attachment guidance for the agent on how to use this store. Rendered into the memory section of the system prompt. Max 4096 chars.

mount\_path: optional stringо‚љ

Filesystem path where the store is mounted in the session container, e.g. /mnt/memory/user-preferences. Derived from the store's name. Output-only.

name: optional stringо‚љ

Display name of the memory store, snapshotted at attach time. Later edits to the store's name do not propagate to this resource.

оЂЄ

#### BetaSessionsThreads

##### [List Session Threads](api/beta/sessions/threads/list.md)

$ ant beta:sessions:threads list

GET/v1/sessions/{session\_id}/threads

##### [Get Session Thread](api/beta/sessions/threads/retrieve.md)

$ ant beta:sessions:threads retrieve

GET/v1/sessions/{session\_id}/threads/{thread\_id}

##### [Archive Session Thread](api/beta/sessions/threads/archive.md)

$ ant beta:sessions:threads archive

POST/v1/sessions/{session\_id}/threads/{thread\_id}/archive

##### ModelsExpand Collapse

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_session\_thread: object { id, agent, archived\_at, 8 more } о‚љ

An execution thread within a `session`. Each session has one primary thread plus zero or more child threads spawned by the coordinator.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this thread.

оЂЄ

agent: object { id, description, mcp\_servers, 7 more } о‚љ

Resolved `agent` definition for a single `session_thread`. Snapshot of the agent at thread creation time. The multiagent roster is not repeated here; read it from `Session.agent`.

id: stringо‚љ

description: stringо‚љ

stats: object { active\_seconds, duration\_seconds, startup\_seconds } о‚љ

Timing statistics for a session thread.

active\_seconds: optional numberо‚љ

Cumulative time in seconds the thread spent actively running. Excludes idle time.

duration\_seconds: optional numberо‚љ

Elapsed time since thread creation in seconds. For archived threads, frozen at the final update.

startup\_seconds: optional numberо‚љ

Time in seconds for the thread to begin running. Zero for child threads, which start immediately.

output\_tokens: optional numberо‚љ

"user.interrupt"о‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

If absent, interrupts every non-archived thread in a multiagent session (or the primary alone in a single-agent session). If present, interrupts only the named thread.

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_user\_tool\_confirmation\_event: object { id, result, tool\_use\_id, 4 more } о‚љ

A tool confirmation event that approves or denies a pending tool execution.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

оЂЄ

result: "allow" or "deny"о‚љ

UserToolConfirmationResult enum

"allow"о‚љ

"deny"о‚љ

tool\_use\_id: stringо‚љ

The id of the `agent.tool_use` or `agent.mcp_tool_use` event this result corresponds to, which can be found in the last `session.status_idle` [event's](api/beta/sessions/events/list.md) `stop_reason.event_ids` field.

оЂЄ

type: "user.tool\_confirmation"о‚љ

"user.tool\_confirmation"о‚љ

deny\_message: optional stringо‚љ

Optional message providing context for a 'deny' decision. Only allowed when result is 'deny'.

processed\_at: optional stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

session\_thread\_id: optional stringо‚љ

When set, the confirmation routes to this subagent's thread rather than the primary. Echo this from the `session_thread_id` on the `agent.tool_use` or `agent.mcp_tool_use` event that prompted the approval.

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_agent\_custom\_tool\_use\_event: object { id, input, name, 3 more } о‚љ

Event emitted when the agent calls a custom tool. The session goes idle until the client sends a `user.custom_tool_result` event with the result.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

input: map[unknown]о‚љ

Input parameters for the tool call.

name: stringо‚љ

Name of the custom tool being called.

processed\_at: stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

оЂЄ

type: "agent.custom\_tool\_use"о‚љ

"agent.custom\_tool\_use"о‚љ

session\_thread\_id: optional stringо‚љ

When set, this event was cross-posted from a subagent's thread to surface its custom tool use on the primary thread's stream. Empty on the thread's own events. Echo this on a `user.custom_tool_result` event to route the result back.

beta\_managed\_agents\_session\_error\_event: object { id, error, processed\_at, type } о‚љ

An error event indicating a problem occurred during session execution.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

оЂЄ

error: [BetaManagedAgentsUnknownError](api/beta.md) { message, retry\_status, type }  or [BetaManagedAgentsModelOverloadedError](api/beta.md) { message, retry\_status, type }  or [BetaManagedAgentsModelRateLimitedError](api/beta.md) { message, retry\_status, type }  or 5 moreо‚љ

An unknown or unexpected error occurred during session execution. A fallback variant; clients that don't recognize a new error code can match on `retry_status` and `message` alone.

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_unknown\_error: object { message, retry\_status, type } о‚љ

An unknown or unexpected error occurred during session execution. A fallback variant; clients that don't recognize a new error code can match on `retry_status` and `message` alone.

message: stringо‚љ

Human-readable error description.

оЂЄ

"retrying"о‚љ

Emitted when a subagent is spawned as a new thread. Written to the parent thread's output stream so clients observing the session see child creation.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

agent\_name: stringо‚љ

Name of the callable agent the thread runs.

processed\_at: stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

Public `sthr_` ID of the newly created thread.

оЂЄ

"session.thread\_created"о‚љ

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_span\_outcome\_evaluation\_start\_event: object { id, iteration, outcome\_id, 2 more } о‚љ

Emitted when an outcome evaluation cycle begins.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

iteration: numberо‚љ

0-indexed revision cycle. 0 is the first evaluation; 1 is the re-evaluation after the first revision; etc.

outcome\_id: stringо‚љ

The `outc_` ID of the outcome being evaluated.

processed\_at: stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

оЂЄ

type: "span.outcome\_evaluation\_start"о‚љ

"span.outcome\_evaluation\_start"о‚љ

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_span\_outcome\_evaluation\_end\_event: object { id, explanation, iteration, 6 more } о‚љ

Emitted when an outcome evaluation cycle completes. Carries the verdict and aggregate token usage. A verdict of `needs_revision` means another evaluation cycle follows; `satisfied`, `max_iterations_reached`, `failed`, or `interrupted` are terminal вЂ” no further evaluation cycles follow.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

explanation: stringо‚љ

Human-readable explanation of the verdict. For `needs_revision`, describes which criteria failed and why.

iteration: numberо‚љ

0-indexed revision cycle, matching the corresponding `span.outcome_evaluation_start`.

outcome\_evaluation\_start\_id: stringо‚љ

The id of the corresponding `span.outcome_evaluation_start` event.

outcome\_id: stringо‚љ

The `outc_` ID of the outcome being evaluated.

processed\_at: stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

result: stringо‚љ

Evaluation verdict. 'satisfied': criteria met, session goes idle. 'needs\_revision': criteria not met, another revision cycle follows. 'max\_iterations\_reached': evaluation budget exhausted with criteria still unmet вЂ” one final acknowledgment turn follows before the session goes idle, but no further evaluation runs. 'failed': grader determined the rubric does not apply to the deliverables. 'interrupted': user sent an interrupt while evaluation was in progress.

оЂЄ

type: "span.outcome\_evaluation\_end"о‚љ

"span.outcome\_evaluation\_end"о‚љ

оЂЄ

usage: object { cache\_creation\_input\_tokens, cache\_read\_input\_tokens, input\_tokens, 2 more } о‚љ

Token usage for a single model request.

cache\_creation\_input\_tokens: numberо‚љ

Tokens used to create prompt cache in this request.

cache\_read\_input\_tokens: numberо‚љ

Tokens read from prompt cache in this request.

input\_tokens: numberо‚љ

Input tokens consumed by this request.

output\_tokens: numberо‚љ

Output tokens generated by this request.

оЂЄ

speed: optional "standard" or "fast"о‚љ

Inference speed mode. `fast` provides significantly faster output token generation at premium pricing. Not all models support `fast`; invalid combinations are rejected at create time.

"standard"о‚љ

"fast"о‚љ

оЂЄ

"fast"о‚љ

beta\_managed\_agents\_span\_outcome\_evaluation\_ongoing\_event: object { id, iteration, outcome\_id, 2 more } о‚љ

Periodic heartbeat emitted while an outcome evaluation cycle is in progress. Distinguishes 'evaluation is actively running' from 'evaluation is stuck' between the corresponding `span.outcome_evaluation_start` and `span.outcome_evaluation_end` events.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

iteration: numberо‚љ

0-indexed revision cycle, matching the corresponding `span.outcome_evaluation_start`.

outcome\_id: stringо‚љ

beta\_managed\_agents\_user\_define\_outcome\_event: object { id, description, max\_iterations, 4 more } о‚љ

Echo of a `user.define_outcome` input event. Carries the server-generated `outcome_id` that subsequent `span.outcome_evaluation_*` events reference.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

description: stringо‚љ

What the agent should produce. Copied from the input event.

max\_iterations: numberо‚љ

Evaluate-then-revise cycles before giving up. Default 3, max 20.

outcome\_id: stringо‚љ

Server-generated `outc_` ID for this outcome. Referenced by `span.outcome_evaluation_*` events and the session's `outcome_evaluations` list.

processed\_at: stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

оЂЄ

The agent is idle waiting on one or more blocking user-input events (tool confirmation, custom tool result, etc.). Resolving all of them transitions the session back to running.

event\_ids: array of stringо‚љ

The ids of events the agent is blocked on. Resolving fewer than all re-emits `session.status_idle` with the remainder.

оЂЄ

type: "requires\_action"о‚љ

"requires\_action"о‚љ

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_session\_retries\_exhausted: object { type } о‚љ

The turn ended because the retry budget was exhausted (`max_iterations` hit or an error escalated to `retry_status: 'exhausted'`).

оЂЄ

type: "retries\_exhausted"о‚љ

"retries\_exhausted"о‚љ

оЂЄ

type: "session.thread\_status\_idle"о‚љ

"session.thread\_status\_idle"о‚љ

beta\_managed\_agents\_session\_thread\_status\_terminated\_event: object { id, agent\_name, processed\_at, 2 more } о‚љ

A session thread has terminated and will accept no further input. Emitted on the thread's own stream and cross-posted to the primary stream for child threads.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

agent\_name: stringо‚љ

Name of the agent the thread runs.

processed\_at: stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

session\_thread\_id: stringо‚љ

Public sthr\_ ID of the thread that terminated.

оЂЄ

type: "session.thread\_status\_terminated"о‚љ

"session.thread\_status\_terminated"о‚љ

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_user\_tool\_result\_event: object { id, tool\_use\_id, type, 4 more } о‚љ

Event sent by the client providing the result of an agent-toolset tool execution. Only valid on `self_hosted` environments, where sandbox-routed tools are executed by the client rather than the server.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

tool\_use\_id: stringо‚љ

The id of the `agent.tool_use` event this result corresponds to, which can be found in the last `session.status_idle` [event's](api/beta/sessions/events/list.md) `stop_reason.event_ids` field.

оЂЄ

type: "user.tool\_result"о‚љ

"user.tool\_result"о‚љ

The session's full metadata bag after the update. Present when the update set non-empty metadata; absent when metadata was unchanged or cleared to empty.

title: optional stringо‚љ

The session's new title. Present only when the update changed it.

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_system\_message\_event: object { id, content, type, processed\_at } о‚љ

A mid-conversation system message event. Carries system-role content that is appended to the session as a `role: "system"` turn.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this event.

оЂЄ

content: array of [BetaManagedAgentsSystemContentBlock](api/beta.md) { text, type } о‚љ

System content blocks. Text-only.

The text content.

оЂЄ

type: "text"о‚љ

"text"о‚љ

оЂЄ

type: "system.message"о‚љ

"system.message"о‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

#### BetaSessionsThreadsEvents

##### [List Session Thread Events](api/beta/sessions/threads/events/list.md)

$ ant beta:sessions:threads:events list

GET/v1/sessions/{session\_id}/threads/{thread\_id}/events

##### [Stream Session Thread Events](api/beta/sessions/threads/events/stream.md)

$ ant beta:sessions:threads:events stream

GET/v1/sessions/{session\_id}/threads/{thread\_id}/stream

#### BetaDeployments

##### [Create Deployment](api/beta/deployments/create.md)

$ ant beta:deployments create

POST/v1/deployments

##### [List Deployments](api/beta/deployments/list.md)

$ ant beta:deployments list

GET/v1/deployments

##### [Get Deployment](api/beta/deployments/retrieve.md)

$ ant beta:deployments retrieve

GET/v1/deployments/{deployment\_id}

##### [Update Deployment](api/beta/deployments/update.md)

$ ant beta:deployments update

POST/v1/deployments/{deployment\_id}

##### [Archive Deployment](api/beta/deployments/archive.md)

$ ant beta:deployments archive

POST/v1/deployments/{deployment\_id}/archive

##### [Run Deployment Now](api/beta/deployments/run.md)

$ ant beta:deployments run

POST/v1/deployments/{deployment\_id}/run

##### [Pause Deployment](api/beta/deployments/pause.md)

$ ant beta:deployments pause

POST/v1/deployments/{deployment\_id}/pause

##### [Unpause Deployment](api/beta/deployments/unpause.md)

$ ant beta:deployments unpause

POST/v1/deployments/{deployment\_id}/unpause

##### ModelsExpand Collapse

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_agent\_archived\_deployment\_paused\_reason\_error: object { type } о‚љ

The deployment's agent was archived.

оЂЄ

type: "agent\_archived\_error"о‚љ

"agent\_archived\_error"о‚љ

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_cron\_schedule: object { expression, timezone, type, 2 more } о‚љ

5-field POSIX cron schedule with computed runtime timestamps.

expression: stringо‚љ

5-field POSIX cron expression: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week (e.g., "0 9 \* \* 1-5" for weekdays at 9am). Day-of-week is 0-7 where 0 and 7 both mean Sunday. Extended cron syntax - seconds or year fields, and the special characters L, W, #, and ? - is not supported, nor are predefined shortcuts (@daily).

timezone: stringо‚љ

IANA timezone identifier (e.g., "America/Los\_Angeles", "UTC").

оЂЄ

type: "cron"о‚љ

"cron"о‚љ

last\_run\_at: optional stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

upcoming\_runs\_at: optional array of stringо‚љ

Up to 5 timestamps of upcoming cron occurrences. Non-empty for active and paused deployments (reflects what the schedule would do if unpaused); empty once the deployment is archived (`archived_at` set). Each fire is offset by a small per-schedule jitter, so a run will actually start at or shortly after its listed time.

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_cron\_schedule\_params: object { expression, timezone, type } о‚љ

5-field POSIX cron schedule. Literal wall-clock matching in the configured timezone.

expression: stringо‚љ

5-field POSIX cron expression: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week (e.g., "0 9 \* \* 1-5" for weekdays at 9am). Day-of-week is 0-7 where 0 and 7 both mean Sunday. Extended cron syntax - seconds or year fields, and the special characters L, W, #, and ? - is not supported, nor are predefined shortcuts (@daily).

timezone: stringо‚љ

Required. IANA timezone identifier (e.g., "America/Los\_Angeles", "UTC"). Validated against the IANA timezone database.

оЂЄ

type: "cron"о‚љ

"cron"о‚љ

beta\_managed\_agents\_system\_message\_event\_params: object { content, type } о‚љ

Privileged context for the accompanying turn and all subsequent turns, appended to the session's system context as a `role: "system"` turn rather than replacing the top-level system prompt. At most one per request: it must be the final event and immediately follow the `user.message`, `user.tool_result`, or `user.custom_tool_result` it accompanies. Only supported on models that accept mid-conversation system messages.

оЂЄ

content: array of [BetaManagedAgentsSystemContentBlock](api/beta.md) { text, type } о‚љ

System content blocks to append. Text-only.

text: stringо‚љ

The text content.

оЂЄ

"text"о‚љ

"system.message"о‚љ

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_schedule: object { expression, timezone, type, 2 more } о‚љ

5-field POSIX cron schedule with computed runtime timestamps.

expression: stringо‚љ

5-field POSIX cron expression: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week (e.g., "0 9 \* \* 1-5" for weekdays at 9am). Day-of-week is 0-7 where 0 and 7 both mean Sunday. Extended cron syntax - seconds or year fields, and the special characters L, W, #, and ? - is not supported, nor are predefined shortcuts (@daily).

timezone: stringо‚љ

IANA timezone identifier (e.g., "America/Los\_Angeles", "UTC").

оЂЄ

type: "cron"о‚љ

"cron"о‚љ

last\_run\_at: optional stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

upcoming\_runs\_at: optional array of stringо‚љ

Up to 5 timestamps of upcoming cron occurrences. Non-empty for active and paused deployments (reflects what the schedule would do if unpaused); empty once the deployment is archived (`archived_at` set). Each fire is offset by a small per-schedule jitter, so a run will actually start at or shortly after its listed time.

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_schedule\_params: object { expression, timezone, type } о‚љ

5-field POSIX cron schedule. Literal wall-clock matching in the configured timezone.

expression: stringо‚љ

5-field POSIX cron expression: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week (e.g., "0 9 \* \* 1-5" for weekdays at 9am). Day-of-week is 0-7 where 0 and 7 both mean Sunday. Extended cron syntax - seconds or year fields, and the special characters L, W, #, and ? - is not supported, nor are predefined shortcuts (@daily).

timezone: stringо‚љ

Required. IANA timezone identifier (e.g., "America/Los\_Angeles", "UTC"). Validated against the IANA timezone database.

оЂЄ

type: "cron"о‚љ

"cron"о‚љ

#### BetaDeployment Runs

##### [List Deployment Runs](api/beta/deployment_runs/list.md)

$ ant beta:deployment-runs list

GET/v1/deployment\_runs

##### [Get Deployment Run](api/beta/deployment_runs/retrieve.md)

$ ant beta:deployment-runs retrieve

GET/v1/deployment\_runs/{deployment\_run\_id}

#### BetaVaults

##### [Create Vault](api/beta/vaults/create.md)

$ ant beta:vaults create

POST/v1/vaults

##### [List Vaults](api/beta/vaults/list.md)

$ ant beta:vaults list

GET/v1/vaults

##### [Get Vault](api/beta/vaults/retrieve.md)

$ ant beta:vaults retrieve

GET/v1/vaults/{vault\_id}

##### [Update Vault](api/beta/vaults/update.md)

$ ant beta:vaults update

POST/v1/vaults/{vault\_id}

##### [Delete Vault](api/beta/vaults/delete.md)

$ ant beta:vaults delete

DELETE/v1/vaults/{vault\_id}

##### [Archive Vault](api/beta/vaults/archive.md)

$ ant beta:vaults archive

POST/v1/vaults/{vault\_id}/archive

#### BetaVaultsCredentials

##### [Create Credential](api/beta/vaults/credentials/create.md)

$ ant beta:vaults:credentials create

POST/v1/vaults/{vault\_id}/credentials

##### [List Credentials](api/beta/vaults/credentials/list.md)

$ ant beta:vaults:credentials list

GET/v1/vaults/{vault\_id}/credentials

##### [Get Credential](api/beta/vaults/credentials/retrieve.md)

$ ant beta:vaults:credentials retrieve

GET/v1/vaults/{vault\_id}/credentials/{credential\_id}

##### [Update Credential](api/beta/vaults/credentials/update.md)

$ ant beta:vaults:credentials update

POST/v1/vaults/{vault\_id}/credentials/{credential\_id}

##### [Delete Credential](api/beta/vaults/credentials/delete.md)

$ ant beta:vaults:credentials delete

DELETE/v1/vaults/{vault\_id}/credentials/{credential\_id}

##### [Archive Credential](api/beta/vaults/credentials/archive.md)

$ ant beta:vaults:credentials archive

POST/v1/vaults/{vault\_id}/credentials/{credential\_id}/archive

##### [Validate Credential](api/beta/vaults/credentials/mcp_oauth_validate.md)

$ ant beta:vaults:credentials mcp-oauth-validate

POST/v1/vaults/{vault\_id}/credentials/{credential\_id}/mcp\_oauth\_validate

#### BetaMemory Stores

##### [Create a memory store](api/beta/memory_stores/create.md)

$ ant beta:memory-stores create

POST/v1/memory\_stores

##### [List memory stores](api/beta/memory_stores/list.md)

$ ant beta:memory-stores list

GET/v1/memory\_stores

##### [Retrieve a memory store](api/beta/memory_stores/retrieve.md)

$ ant beta:memory-stores retrieve

GET/v1/memory\_stores/{memory\_store\_id}

##### [Update a memory store](api/beta/memory_stores/update.md)

$ ant beta:memory-stores update

POST/v1/memory\_stores/{memory\_store\_id}

##### [Delete a memory store](api/beta/memory_stores/delete.md)

$ ant beta:memory-stores delete

DELETE/v1/memory\_stores/{memory\_store\_id}

##### [Archive a memory store](api/beta/memory_stores/archive.md)

$ ant beta:memory-stores archive

POST/v1/memory\_stores/{memory\_store\_id}/archive

##### ModelsExpand Collapse

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_deleted\_memory\_store: object { id, type } о‚љ

Confirmation that a `memory_store` was deleted.

id: stringо‚љ

ID of the deleted memory store (a `memstore_...` identifier). The store and all its memories and versions are no longer retrievable.

оЂЄ

type: "memory\_store\_deleted"о‚љ

"memory\_store\_deleted"о‚љ

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_memory\_store: object { id, created\_at, name, 5 more } о‚љ

A `memory_store`: a named container for agent memories, scoped to a workspace. Attach a store to a session via `resources[]` to mount it as a directory the agent can read and write.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for the memory store (a `memstore_...` tagged ID). Use this when attaching the store to a session, or in the `{memory_store_id}` path parameter of subsequent calls.

created\_at: stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

name: stringо‚љ

Human-readable name for the store. 1вЂ“255 characters. The store's mount-path slug under `/mnt/memory/` is derived from this name.

оЂЄ

type: "memory\_store"о‚љ

"memory\_store"о‚љ

updated\_at: stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

archived\_at: optional stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

description: optional stringо‚љ

Free-text description of what the store contains, up to 1024 characters. Included in the agent's system prompt when the store is attached, so word it to be useful to the agent. Empty string when unset.

metadata: optional map[string]о‚љ

Arbitrary key-value tags for your own bookkeeping (such as the end user a store belongs to). Up to 16 pairs; keys 1вЂ“64 characters; values up to 512 characters. Returned on retrieve/list but not filterable.

#### BetaMemory StoresMemories

##### [Create a memory](api/beta/memory_stores/memories/create.md)

$ ant beta:memory-stores:memories create

POST/v1/memory\_stores/{memory\_store\_id}/memories

##### [List memories](api/beta/memory_stores/memories/list.md)

$ ant beta:memory-stores:memories list

GET/v1/memory\_stores/{memory\_store\_id}/memories

##### [Retrieve a memory](api/beta/memory_stores/memories/retrieve.md)

$ ant beta:memory-stores:memories retrieve

GET/v1/memory\_stores/{memory\_store\_id}/memories/{memory\_id}

##### [Update a memory](api/beta/memory_stores/memories/update.md)

$ ant beta:memory-stores:memories update

POST/v1/memory\_stores/{memory\_store\_id}/memories/{memory\_id}

##### [Delete a memory](api/beta/memory_stores/memories/delete.md)

$ ant beta:memory-stores:memories delete

DELETE/v1/memory\_stores/{memory\_store\_id}/memories/{memory\_id}

##### ModelsExpand Collapse

beta\_managed\_agents\_conflict\_error: object { type, message } о‚љ

оЂЄ

type: "conflict\_error"о‚љ

"conflict\_error"о‚љ

message: optional stringо‚љ

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_content\_sha256\_precondition: object { type, content\_sha256 } о‚љ

Optimistic-concurrency precondition: the update applies only if the memory's stored `content_sha256` equals the supplied value. On mismatch, the request returns `memory_precondition_failed_error` (HTTP 409); re-read the memory and retry against the fresh state. If the precondition fails but the stored state already exactly matches the requested `content` and `path`, the server returns 200 instead of 409.

оЂЄ

type: "content\_sha256"о‚љ

"content\_sha256"о‚љ

content\_sha256: optional stringо‚љ

Expected `content_sha256` of the stored memory (64 lowercase hexadecimal characters). Typically the `content_sha256` returned by a prior read or list call. Because the server applies no content normalization, clients can also compute this locally as the SHA-256 of the UTF-8 content bytes.

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_deleted\_memory: object { id, type } о‚љ

Tombstone returned by [Delete a memory](api/beta/memory_stores/memories/delete.md). The memory's version history persists and remains listable via [List memory versions](api/beta/memory_stores/memory_versions/list.md) until the store itself is deleted.

id: stringо‚љ

ID of the deleted memory (a `mem_...` value).

оЂЄ

type: "memory\_deleted"о‚љ

"memory\_deleted"о‚љ

beta\_managed\_agents\_memory: object { id, content\_sha256, content\_size\_bytes, 7 more } о‚љ

A `memory` object: a single text document at a hierarchical path inside a memory store. The `content` field is populated when `view=full` and `null` when `view=basic`; the `content_size_bytes` and `content_sha256` fields are always populated so sync clients can diff without fetching content. Memories are addressed by their `mem_...` ID; the path is the create key and can be changed via update.

id: stringо‚љ

Unique identifier for this memory (a `mem_...` value). Stable across renames; use this ID, not the path, to read, update, or delete the memory.

content\_sha256: stringо‚љ

Lowercase hex SHA-256 digest of the UTF-8 `content` bytes (64 characters). The server applies no normalization, so clients can compute the same hash locally for staleness checks and as the value for a `content_sha256` precondition on update. Always populated, regardless of `view`.

content\_size\_bytes: numberо‚љ

Size of `content` in bytes (the UTF-8 plaintext length). Always populated, regardless of `view`.

created\_at: stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

memory\_store\_id: stringо‚љ

ID of the memory store this memory belongs to (a `memstore_...` value).

memory\_version\_id: stringо‚љ

ID of the `memory_version` representing this memory's current content (a `memver_...` value). This is the authoritative head pointer; `memory_version` objects do not carry an `is_latest` flag, so compare against this field instead. Enumerate the full history via [List memory versions](api/beta/memory_stores/memory_versions/list.md).

path: stringо‚љ

Hierarchical path of the memory within the store, e.g. `/projects/foo/notes.md`. Always starts with `/`. Paths are case-sensitive and unique within a store. Maximum 1,024 bytes.

оЂЄ

type: "memory"о‚љ

"memory"о‚љ

updated\_at: stringо‚љ

A timestamp in RFC 3339 format

content: optional stringо‚љ

The memory's UTF-8 text content. Populated when `view=full`; `null` when `view=basic`. Maximum 100 kB (102,400 bytes).

оЂЄ

beta\_managed\_agents\_memory\_list\_item: [BetaManagedAgentsMemory](api/beta.md) { id, content\_sha256, content\_size\_bytes, 7 more }  or [BetaManagedAgentsMemoryPrefix](api/beta.md) { path, type } о‚љ

One item in a [List memories](api/beta/memory_stores/memories/list.md) response: either a `memory` object or, when `depth` is set, a `memory_prefix` rollup marker.

оЂЄ

A `memory` object: a single text document at a hierarchical path inside a memory store. The `content` field is populated when `view=full` and `null` when `view=basic`; the `content_size_bytes` and `content_sha256` fields are always populated so sync clients can diff without fetching content. Memories are addressed by their `mem_...` ID; the path is the create key and can be changed via update.
